title: Daily Briefing — Jun 08
headline: Volatility is waking up, tech's leading but rotation churn is everywhere — patience rules today.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us: VIX is elevated at 21.51, trending above its 20 MA (17.12), and Fear & Greed has cratered from last month's greed (67.3) to a steady 'fear' at 42.1. That's your recipe for chop, not runaway bull. SPY is still below the daily 20 MA (746.55), so no long chases here — less trades = more wins.

On sector rotation, XLK (Technology) is still the only clear leader (RS=120.4, Mom=100.7), but everything else is cycling through 'improving' or 'lagging'. Industrials (XLI), Healthcare (XLV), and Financials (XLF) are showing improving momentum — keep an eye. Utilities, Energy, and Staples are lagging hard and risk is HIGH here, so step lightly.

Contrarian play? Retail support is thin, dark pool signals are muted, and most tickers are in 'approaching' or 'watching' status with very few actual triggers. AAPL, XLK, and DELL are above their 20 MAs — if you must play, stick with the real strength. With VIX up and SPY still below mean reversion, don't try to be a hero. Patience, react, don't predict.
callout_body:
SPY: 746.55 daily MA (the pivot — no long exposure below)
XLK: 183.27 daily MA (tech's support)
VIX: 21.51 — above 20 MA, volatility regime confirmed
XLF: 51.55 daily MA (financials must hold)
Fear & Greed: 42.1 — defensive posture
MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, META — all still below their respective 20 MAs, avoid chasing
COIN: 185.37 daily MA (resistance), approaching status
action_items: If SPY closes below 746.55, maintain defensive exposure; no new longs until reclaimed.
Watch Financials (XLF) at 51.55 — if support breaks, reduce risk across portfolio.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price — Volatility's the only headline worth following today.
